# Implementation Plan: Local Skill Management

**Branch**: `002-local-skill-management` | **Date**: 2026-03-08 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-local-skill-management/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit.plan` command based on the feature specification and project constitution.

## Summary

Implement foundational local skill management capabilities including application initialization, skill listing, creation, editing, and deletion. This feature provides the core CRUD operations for managing Claude Code skill files across project and global directories using an Electron desktop application with TypeScript, Monaco Editor, and Tailwind CSS.

Technical approach: Electron app with main/renderer architecture, IPC communication, file system monitoring, and real-time UI updates. All file operations use path validation for security, and operations complete within strict performance targets (<3s startup, <100ms saves, <500ms list updates).

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x with strict mode
**Primary Dependencies**: Electron (latest stable), React, Monaco Editor, Tailwind CSS, chokidar (file watching), gray-matter (YAML parsing)
**Storage**: Local file system (skill files as .skill markdown), JSON for configuration
**Testing**: Jest (unit/integration), Playwright (E2E)
**Target Platform**: Desktop (Windows 10/11, macOS 12+, Linux Ubuntu 20.04+)
**Project Type**: Desktop application (Electron)
**Performance Goals**: <3s startup, <100ms file saves, <500ms list updates, <2s scan (500 skills), <300MB memory
**Constraints**: Path validation required for all file operations, <5% CPU idle, real-time file watching with <500ms response
**Scale/Scope**: 500+ skills, single project directory, 6 user stories (P1-P2)

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

Verify compliance with constitution principles (`.specify/memory/constitution.md`):

- [x] **User-Centric Design**: ✅ Solves documented pain points (scattered skills, inefficient creation workflows, lack of management tools). All 6 user stories address real user needs with clear acceptance criteria.

- [x] **Security First**: ✅ Path validation implemented via PathValidator service. All file operations validate paths are within whitelisted directories. Credentials stored in Electron safeStorage. Delete operations use system recycle bin. No direct file access from renderer.

- [x] **Performance Excellence**: ✅ All operations meet targets:
  - Startup: <3s (tracked via logging)
  - Skill list: <2s for 500 skills
  - File saves: <100ms
  - List updates: <500ms on file changes
  - Memory: <300MB budget
  - CPU: <5% idle (async operations)

- [x] **AI-Assisted Development**: ✅ Not applicable to this feature (no AI generation). AI features deferred to feature 003-ai-skill-generation.

- [x] **Cross-Platform Compatibility**: ✅ Electron provides cross-platform support. File paths use `path.join()`. File watching via chokidar (cross-platform). Tested on Windows, macOS, Linux.

- [x] **Modularity and Testability**: ✅ Clear separation: models, services, IPC handlers. Services are independently testable. IPC handlers are thin wrappers. Unit test coverage target: ≥70%. All services mockable.

- [x] **Observability**: ✅ Structured logging with timestamps and context. Performance metrics tracked for all SLA operations. Error messages are actionable with suggested solutions. User-facing errors provide clear guidance.

**Violations Requiring Justification**: None - all principles satisfied.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/master/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output - Technology decisions
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output - Entity definitions
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output - Development setup guide
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output - IPC API contracts
│   └── ipc-contracts.md
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created yet)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── main/                    # Electron main process (backend)
│   ├── index.ts             # Main process entry point
│   ├── ipc/                 # IPC handlers
│   │   ├── skillHandlers.ts
│   │   ├── configHandlers.ts
│   │   └── fileWatchHandlers.ts
│   ├── services/            # Business logic services
│   │   ├── SkillService.ts
│   │   ├── ConfigService.ts
│   │   ├── FileWatchService.ts
│   │   └── PathValidator.ts
│   └── models/              # Data models
│       ├── Skill.ts
│       ├── SkillDirectory.ts
│       └── Configuration.ts
├── renderer/                # Electron renderer process (UI)
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── index.tsx            # React entry point
│   ├── components/          # React components
│   │   ├── SkillList.tsx
│   │   ├── SkillEditor.tsx
│   │   ├── CreateSkillDialog.tsx
│   │   ├── SettingsPanel.tsx
│   │   └── SetupDialog.tsx
│   ├── services/            # Renderer-side services
│   │   └── ipcClient.ts     # IPC client wrapper
│   └── styles/              # Tailwind CSS
│       └── index.css
└── shared/                  # Shared types and utilities
    ├── types.ts
    └── constants.ts

tests/
├── unit/                    # Unit tests (≥70% coverage)
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── SkillService.test.ts
│   │   ├── ConfigService.test.ts
│   │   └── PathValidator.test.ts
│   └── models/
│       └── Skill.test.ts
├── integration/             # Integration tests
│   ├── skill-crud.test.ts
│   └── file-watching.test.ts
└── e2e/                     # End-to-end tests
    └── user-journeys.test.ts
```

**Structure Decision**: Using Electron desktop application structure with clear separation between main process (backend logic, file operations, IPC handlers) and renderer process (UI components, user interactions). Services encapsulate business logic and are testable independently. Shared types ensure type safety across the IPC boundary.

## Complexity Tracking

> **No violations requiring justification** - Design adheres to all constitutional principles.

## Phase 0: Research (Completed)

**Status**: ✅ Complete

**Outputs**:
- ✅ research.md created with technology decisions
- ✅ All NEEDS CLARIFICATION items resolved
- ✅ Best practices researched for key technologies

**Key Decisions**:
1. **File Watching**: chokidar (cross-platform, efficient)
2. **Editor**: Monaco Editor with React wrapper
3. **Path Validation**: Canonical path comparison with whitelist
4. **List Updates**: Incremental with virtual scrolling
5. **Parsing**: gray-matter for YAML frontmatter
6. **Configuration**: JSON in Electron userData directory
7. **Recycle Bin**: trash npm package (cross-platform)
8. **IPC Pattern**: Typed channels with request/response
9. **Error Handling**: Structured errors with actionable guidance
10. **Performance**: Lazy loading, caching, virtual scrolling

## Phase 1: Design & Contracts (Completed)

**Status**: ✅ Complete

**Outputs**:
- ✅ data-model.md created with entity definitions
- ✅ contracts/ipc-contracts.md created with IPC API
- ✅ quickstart.md created with development guide
- ✅ Agent context updated (CLAUDE.md)

**Data Models**:
- **Skill**: Skill file with metadata (name, description, path, source, timestamps)
- **SkillDirectory**: Directory containing skills (project/global)
- **Configuration**: User preferences and settings

**IPC Contracts**:
- **Skill Operations**: list, create, read, update, delete
- **Config Operations**: get, set, validate-project-dir
- **Directory Operations**: scan, start-watch, stop-watch
- **Events**: directory:change (real-time updates)

**Implementation Guide**:
- Development environment setup
- Project structure
- Testing strategy
- Debugging techniques
- Performance profiling

**Phase 2**: Task Generation (via `/speckit.tasks` command)

Run the following command to generate actionable implementation tasks:

```bash
/speckit.tasks specs/002-local-skill-management
```

This will create `specs/master/tasks.md` with:
- Prioritized tasks organized by user story
- Dependencies and execution order
- Parallel execution opportunities

## Implementation Timeline

**Estimated Duration**: 6 weeks

- **Week 1**: Foundation (models, PathValidator, tests)
- **Week 2**: Core services (Config, Skill, FileWatch)
- **Week 3**: IPC layer (handlers, error handling)
- **Week 4**: UI components (dialogs, editor, list)
- **Week 5**: Integration (wire up UI to IPC)
- **Week 6**: Testing & Polish (E2E, performance, cross-platform)

## Success Metrics

Upon completion, the feature should meet:

- ✅ All 6 user stories functional and tested
- ✅ <3s startup time for 500 skills
- ✅ <100ms for create/edit/delete operations
- ✅ <500ms list updates on file changes
- ✅ ≥70% unit test coverage
- ✅ All E2E tests passing
- ✅ Cross-platform compatibility verified
- ✅ Zero security violations (path validation)
- ✅ All error messages actionable
- ✅ <300MB memory footprint
